The Artist

Biography


Born and raised in Porto, Paula Morna Dória is one of the most distinguished opera sopranos of her generation — a voice forged across three continents through decades of elite training and crowned with the world's most prestigious prizes, including the New York Music Award 2026.

She holds a Bachelor of Music from the Escola Superior de Música e Artes do Espetáculo, Porto (1995, with 19/20 in Voice), a Master of Music in Vocal Performance from Indiana University School of Music, Bloomington, USA (1998, under Virginia Zeani and Klara Barlow), and a Doctor of Musical Arts (Vocal Performance) from Rutgers University, New Jersey (2011, 19/20) — the latter funded by the Foundation for Science and Technology.

Her doctoral thesis, "Berlioz and Les nuits d'été: Aspects of Reception and Aesthetics", written and defended in English at Rutgers University, is a landmark of musicological scholarship. She also holds a degree in Philosophy from the University of Porto.

Paula has performed on the stages of the Teatro Nacional de São Carlos (Lisbon), Avery Fisher Hall / Lincoln Center (New York), Carnegie Weill Hall (New York), Casa da Música (Porto), Centro Cultural de Belém, and prestigious venues across Europe and the United States. Her conductors include Lawrence Foster, Donato Renzetti, João Paulo Santos, Thomas Netopil, and Frédérique Chaslin.

She is the only female vocal coach at the Old School of Singing in Lucca, Italy, under renowned tenor Giancarlo Monsalve. Her MajorMinor studio in Lisbon cultivates the next generation of operatic voices.

Academic Excellence

Pedagogy & Academia


Paula Morna Dória's pedagogical practice is inseparable from her performing career. Holding a Doctor of Musical Arts from Rutgers University and a Master of Music from Indiana University, she brings exceptional scholarly depth to the vocal arts.

For over a decade she served as Assistant Professor at the University of Évora's Department of Performing Arts, holding chairs in Voice, Singing, and Vocal Technique. She is the sole female vocal coach at the internationally acclaimed Old School of Singing in Lucca, Italy.

Enquiries for Masterclasses, Institutional Partnerships, and Private Coaching are welcomed via the contact form.

Jury Member — International Competitions

Hans-Gabor Belvedere · SOI Fiorenza Cedolins

Invited as jury member to two of the world's premier singing competitions — the Hans-Gabor Belvedere (semi-final at Teatro Nacional de São Carlos) and the SOI Fiorenza Cedolins competition.

2020

Jury Member — Concurso Internacional de Canto Vincerò

Portugal · alongside Ivan von Kalmthout, Artistic Director TNSC

Invited jury member at this international Portuguese singing competition, alongside the incoming Artistic Director of the Teatro Nacional de São Carlos.

2003–04

Bellini International Vocal Competition

Ragusa, Italy → Carnegie Weill Hall, New York

Winner of the Best Chamber Music Singer prize — leading to her international début recital at Carnegie Weill Hall, New York in 2004, NY agency representation, and citation in the prestigious Musical América yearbook.

Best Chamber Music Singer
1999

Search for a Star — Indianapolis

Indianapolis Symphony Orchestra · USA

Finalist in this prestigious American vocal competition, performing operatic arias with the Indianapolis Symphony Orchestra under the baton of Marvin Hamlisch, President of the Jury.
